title: Manual
text: arkhe package provides set S4 classes archaeological data matrices. new classes represent different special types matrix. CountMatrix represents absolute frequency data, OccurrenceMatrix represents co-occurrence matrix, CompositionMatrix represents relative frequency data, IncidenceMatrix represents presence/absence data, StratigraphicMatrix represents stratigraphic relationships. assumes keep data tidy: variable (taxon/type) must saved column observation (assemblage/sample) must saved row. Note missing values allowed. internal structure S4 classes implemented arkhe depicted UML class diagram following figure. UML class diagram S4 classes structure.

title: Manual
text: denote \(m \times p\) count matrix \(= \left[ a_{ij} \right] ~\forall \\left[ 1,m \right], j \\left[ 1,p \right]\) row column sums: \[\begin{align}  a_{\cdot} = \sum_{j = 1}^{p} a_{ij} &&  a_{\cdot j} = \sum_{= 1}^{m} a_{ij} &&  a_{\cdot \cdot} = \sum_{= 1}^{m} \sum_{j = 1}^{p} a_{ij} &&  \forall a_{ij} \\mathbb{N} \end{align}\]

title: Manual
text: frequency matrix represents relative abundances. denote \(m \times p\) frequency matrix \(B = \left[ b_{ij} \right] ~\forall \\left[ 1,m \right], j \\left[ 1,p \right]\) row column sums: \[\begin{align}  b_{\cdot} = \sum_{j = 1}^{p} b_{ij} = 1 &&  b_{\cdot j} = \sum_{= 1}^{m} b_{ij} &&  b_{\cdot \cdot} = \sum_{= 1}^{m} \sum_{j = 1}^{p} b_{ij} &&  \forall b_{ij} \\left[ 0,1 \right] \end{align}\]

title: Manual
text: co-occurrence matrix symmetric matrix zeros main diagonal, works many times pairs taxa occur together least one sample. \(p \times p\) co-occurrence matrix \(D = \left[ d_{,j} \right] ~\forall ,j \\left[ 1,p \right]\) defined \(m \times p\) abundance matrix \(= \left[ a_{x,y} \right] ~\forall x \\left[ 1,m \right], y \\left[ 1,p \right]\) : \[ d_{,j} = \sum_{x = 1}^{m} \bigcap_{y = }^{j} a_{xy} \] row column sums: \[\begin{align}   d_{\cdot} = \sum_{j \geqslant }^{p} d_{ij} &&   d_{\cdot j} = \sum_{\leqslant j}^{p} d_{ij} &&   d_{\cdot \cdot} = \sum_{= 1}^{p} \sum_{j \geqslant }^{p} d_{ij} &&   \forall d_{ij} \\mathbb{N} \end{align}\]

title: Manual
text: denote \(m \times p\) incidence matrix \(C = \left[ c_{ij} \right] ~\forall \\left[ 1,m \right], j \\left[ 1,p \right]\) row column sums: \[\begin{align}  c_{\cdot} = \sum_{j = 1}^{p} c_{ij} &&  c_{\cdot j} = \sum_{= 1}^{m} c_{ij} &&  c_{\cdot \cdot} = \sum_{= 1}^{m} \sum_{j = 1}^{p} c_{ij} &&  \forall c_{ij} \\lbrace 0,1 \rbrace \end{align}\]

title: Manual
text: arkhe uses coercing mechanisms (validation methods) data type conversions:
code: ### Create a count matrix A0 <- matrix(data = sample(0:10, 100, TRUE), nrow = 10, ncol = 10)  ### Coerce to absolute frequencies A1 <- as_count(A0)  ### Coerce to relative frequencies B <- as_composition(A1)  ### Row sums are internally stored before coercing to a frequency matrix ### (use get_totals() to get these values) ### This allows to restore the source data A2 <- as_count(B) all(A1 == A2) #> [1] TRUE  ### Coerce to presence/absence C <- as_incidence(A1)  ### Coerce to a co-occurrence matrix D <- as_occurrence(A1)

title: Representation of Archaeological Data
text: collection classes represent archaeological data. package provides set S4 classes represent different special types matrix (absolute/relative frequency, presence/absence data, co-occurrence matrix, etc.) upon package developers can build subclasses. also provides set generic methods (mutators coercion mechanisms) functions (e.g. predicates). addition, classes general interest (e.g. represent stratigraphic relationships) implemented.

title: Representation of Archaeological Data
text: can install released version arkhe CRAN : development version GitHub :
code: install.packages("arkhe") # install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("tesselle/arkhe")

title: Representation of Archaeological Data
text: arkhe provides set S4 classes represent different special types matrix. CountMatrix represents absolute frequency data, OccurrenceMatrix represents co-occurrence matrix, CompositionMatrix represents relative frequency (compositional) data, IncidenceMatrix represents presence/absence data, StratigraphicMatrix represents stratigraphic relationships. assumes keep data tidy: variable (type/taxa) must saved column observation (assemblage/sample) must saved row. new classes simple use inherit base matrix: arkhe uses coercing mechanisms (validation methods) data type conversions: CountMatrix, CompositionMatrix IncidenceMatrix classes special slots: samples replicated measurements/observation, groups group data site/area, dates specify date point estimate assemblage, tqp taq specify chronology assemblage. way chronological information handled somewhat opinionated. Sub-annual precision overkill/meaningless situations: dates assumed expressed years CE stored integers (values coerced .integer() hence truncated towards zero). coercing data.frame *Matrix object, attempt made automatically assign values slots mapping column names. behavior can disabled setting options(arkhe.autodetect = FALSE).
code: ## Load the package library(arkhe) ## Define a count data matrix ## (data will be rounded to zero decimal places, then coerced with as.integer) quanti <- CountMatrix(data = sample(0:10, 100, TRUE), nrow = 10, ncol = 10)  ## Define a logical matrix ## (data will be coerced with as.logical) quali <- IncidenceMatrix(data = sample(0:1, 100, TRUE), nrow = 10, ncol = 10) ## Create a count matrix X <- matrix(data = sample(0:10, 75, TRUE), nrow = 15, ncol = 5)  ## Coerce to absolute frequencies A1 <- as_count(X)  ## Coerce to relative frequencies B <- as_composition(A1)  ## Row sums are internally stored before coercing to a frequency matrix ## (use get_totals() to get these values) ## This allows to restore the source data A2 <- as_count(B) all(A1 == A2) #> [1] TRUE  ## Coerce to presence/absence D <- as_incidence(A1)  ## Coerce to a co-occurrence matrix E <- as_occurrence(A1) Y <- as.data.frame(X) Y$samples <- sample(c("a", "b", "c", "d", "e"), 15, TRUE) Y$groups <- sample(c("A", "B", "C", "D", "E"), 15, TRUE) Y$dates <- sample(1400:1451, 15, TRUE) Y$tpq <- sample(1301:1400, 15, TRUE) # TPQ Y$taq <- sample(1451:1500, 15, TRUE) # TAQ  ## Coerce to a count matrix Z <- as_count(Y)  ## Get groups get_samples(Z) #>  [1] "a" "e" "e" "d" "a" "a" "c" "a" "e" "c" "c" "b" "a" "e" "c"  ## Get groups get_groups(Z) #>  [1] "A" "E" "D" "E" "A" "B" "E" "C" "B" "B" "C" "A" "E" "E" "D"  ## Get dates get_dates(Z) #>  [1] 1407 1423 1412 1416 1427 1445 1421 1448 1400 1419 1415 1407 1423 1446 1415  ## Get chronology get_terminus(Z) #>        tpq  taq #> row1  1367 1465 #> row2  1327 1498 #> row3  1357 1460 #> row4  1342 1486 #> row5  1337 1485 #> row6  1362 1498 #> row7  1354 1459 #> row8  1334 1469 #> row9  1395 1488 #> row10 1330 1457 #> row11 1305 1480 #> row12 1366 1476 #> row13 1374 1488 #> row14 1307 1455 #> row15 1325 1468  ## Summarize summary(Z) #> <AbundanceSummary: 15 x 5> #> A ------------------------------------------------------------------------------ #> * Observations: 3 #> * Variables: 5 #> * Replicated measurements: TRUE #> * Chronology: 1337 - 1485 (year CE) #> B ------------------------------------------------------------------------------ #> * Observations: 3 #> * Variables: 5 #> * Replicated measurements: FALSE #> * Chronology: 1330 - 1498 (year CE) #> C ------------------------------------------------------------------------------ #> * Observations: 2 #> * Variables: 5 #> * Replicated measurements: FALSE #> * Chronology: 1305 - 1480 (year CE) #> D ------------------------------------------------------------------------------ #> * Observations: 2 #> * Variables: 5 #> * Replicated measurements: FALSE #> * Chronology: 1325 - 1468 (year CE) #> E ------------------------------------------------------------------------------ #> * Observations: 5 #> * Variables: 5 #> * Replicated measurements: TRUE #> * Chronology: 1307 - 1498 (year CE) #> --------------------------------------------------------------------------------

text: samples character vector. groups character vector. totals numeric vector giving absolute row sums. dates integer vector specifying date point estimate row. tpq integer vector specifying TPQ row. taq integer vector specifying TAQ row.

title: Archaeological Data — AbundanceMatrix-class
text: code snippets , x AbundanceMatrix object. get_samples(x) get_samples(x) <- value Get set sample names x. get_groups(x) set_groups(x) <- value Get set groups x. get_dates(x) set_dates(x) <- value Get set dates x. value can list components tpq (TPQ - terminus post quem) taq (TAQ - terminus ante quem) numeric vector (point estimate). elements value coerced integer .integer() (hence truncated towards zero).

title: Archaeological Data — AbundanceMatrix-class
text: CountMatrix, CompositionMatrix IncidenceMatrix classes special slots: samples replicated measurements/observation, groups group data site/area, dates specify date point estimate assemblage, tqp taq specify chronology assemblage. coercing data.frame *Matrix object, attempt made automatically assign values slots mapping column names (case insensitive, plural insensitive). behavior can disabled setting options(arkhe.autodetect = FALSE) overrided explicitly specifying columns used as_*().

what: arkhe: Representation of Archaeological Data — arkhe-package
title: arkhe: Representation of Archaeological Data — arkhe-package
text: collection classes represent archaeological data. package provides set S4 classes represent different special types matrix (absolute/relative frequency, presence/absence data, co-occurrence matrix, etc.) upon package developers can build subclasses. also provides set generic methods (mutators coercion mechanisms) functions (e.g. summary statistics, predicates). addition, classes general interest (e.g. represent stratigraphic relationships) implemented.

text: following methods coerce object *Matrix object: Note as_count() rounds numeric values zero decimal places coerces integer .integer(). as_stratigraphy() converts set stratigraphic relationships (edges) stratigraphic (adjacency) matrix. can matrix, list, data.frame: first column/component assumed contain bottom units second top units (adjacency). as_features() converts *Matrix object collection features: data.frame informations extra columns (result may differ according class ).

title: Coerce — coerce
code: ## Create a count matrix A0 <- matrix(data = sample(0:10, 100, TRUE), nrow = 20, ncol = 5)  ## Coerce to absolute frequencies A1 <- as_count(A0)  ## Coerce to relative frequencies B0 <- as_composition(A1)  ## Row sums are internally stored before coercing to relative frequencies ## (use get_totals() to retrieve these values) ## This allows to restore the source data A2 <- as_count(B0) all(A1 == A2) #> [1] TRUE  ## Coerce to presence/absence C0 <- as_incidence(A1)  ## Coerce to a co-occurrence matrix D0 <- as_occurrence(A1)  ## Coerce to an S3 matrix or data.frame X <- as.matrix(A1) all(A0 == X) #> [1] TRUE  Y <- data.frame(A1) head(Y) #>      col1 col2 col3 col4 col5 #> row1    8    0    9    4    9 #> row2    6    7    0    1    3 #> row3    0    6    5    5    3 #> row4    9    8    7    4    4 #> row5    2    1    8    4    9 #> row6    5    7    9    9   10

code: ## Create a data.frame X <- matrix(data = sample(0:10, 50, TRUE), nrow = 10, ncol = 5) Y <- as.data.frame(X)  ## Coerce to a count matrix Z <- as_count(Y)  ## Set/get groups set_samples(Z) <- rep(c("a", "b", "c", "d", "e"), each = 2) get_samples(Z) #>  [1] "a" "a" "b" "b" "c" "c" "d" "d" "e" "e"  ## Set/get groups set_groups(Z) <- rep(c("A", "B"), each = 5) get_groups(Z) #>  [1] "A" "A" "A" "A" "A" "B" "B" "B" "B" "B"  ## Get/get TPQ/TAQ chrono <- list(   tpq = sample(1301:1400, 10, replace = TRUE),   taq = sample(1451:1500, 10, replace = TRUE) ) set_terminus(Z) <- chrono get_terminus(Z) #>        tpq  taq #> row1  1323 1493 #> row2  1386 1459 #> row3  1324 1465 #> row4  1328 1495 #> row5  1382 1461 #> row6  1353 1498 #> row7  1338 1487 #> row8  1302 1474 #> row9  1320 1473 #> row10 1387 1494  ## Collection of features as_features(Z) #>       V1 V2 V3 V4 V5 samples group dates  tpq  taq #> row1   0  4  6  8  1       a     A    NA 1323 1493 #> row2   6  2  0  3  0       a     A    NA 1386 1459 #> row3   4  1  0  0  7       b     A    NA 1324 1465 #> row4   1 10  6  0  4       b     A    NA 1328 1495 #> row5   1  8  5  0 10       c     A    NA 1382 1461 #> row6   2  1  5  6  0       c     B    NA 1353 1498 #> row7   2  7  0  4  1       d     B    NA 1338 1487 #> row8   0  2  4  0  3       d     B    NA 1302 1474 #> row9   8 10  2  6  9       e     B    NA 1320 1473 #> row10  0  6  8 10  9       e     B    NA 1387 1494  ## Summarize data summary(Z) #> <AbundanceSummary: 10 x 5> #> A ------------------------------------------------------------------------------ #> * Observations: 5 #> * Variables: 5 #> * Replicated measurements: TRUE #> * Chronology: 1323 - 1495 (year CE) #> B ------------------------------------------------------------------------------ #> * Observations: 5 #> * Variables: 5 #> * Replicated measurements: TRUE #> * Chronology: 1302 - 1498 (year CE) #> --------------------------------------------------------------------------------
